paper.title: High speed stars: III. Detailed abundances and binary nature of the extreme speed star GHS143

review.summary: Paper presents a detailed spectroscopic analysis of the extreme high-speed star GHS143 using two UVES high-resolution spectra. Two LaTeX tables appear in the source: an abundance table listing derived chemical abundances for 35 species from the November 2023 spectrum, and an uncertainty table breaking down random and systematic (stellar parameter) uncertainties for each element. The paper also mentions that the investigated spectral lines are available in an online table at CDS.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the detailed chemical abundances derived for the high-speed star GHS143 from the UVES spectrum observed in November 2023, including absolute abundances, solar-relative ratios, line counts, and NLTE corrections.

columns.name: Element
columns.meaning: Chemical species (element symbol with ionisation state, e.g. C I, Fe II)

columns.name: Nlin
columns.meaning: Number of spectral lines used to derive the abundance for that species

columns.name: A(X)☉
columns.meaning: Solar reference abundance on the logarithmic scale where A(H)=12

columns.name: A(X)
columns.meaning: Derived absolute abundance of the species for GHS143 on the same logarithmic scale

columns.name: [X/H]
columns.meaning: Abundance relative to hydrogen compared to the solar value: A(X) - A(X)☉

columns.name: σ
columns.meaning: Standard deviation or line-to-line scatter of the abundance measurement

columns.name: [X/Fe]
columns.meaning: Abundance ratio relative to iron compared to the solar value: [X/H] - [Fe/H]

columns.name: σ
columns.meaning: Standard deviation or uncertainty of the [X/Fe] ratio

columns.name: Cor(NLTE)
columns.meaning: Non-local thermodynamic equilibrium correction applied to the abundance

internal_tables.evidence: LaTeX table in the appendix (label tab:abbo) explicitly described as listing abundances derived from the November 2023 UVES spectrum. Referenced in Sec. 3.4 (Abundances) and Appendix A.
internal_tables.comments: Contains 35 rows covering C, N, O, Na, Mg, Al, Si, K, Ca, Sc, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Sr, Y, Zr, Ba, La, Ce, Nd, Sm, Eu, Dy, Er. Some σ and Cor(NLTE) cells are blank where not applicable or not calculated.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Reports the random and systematic uncertainties on the derived abundances for each element, breaking down contributions from signal-to-noise ratio and from variations in stellar parameters (Teff, logg, microturbulence).

columns.name: Ellement
columns.meaning: Chemical species (element symbol with ionisation state). Note: header is misspelled 'Ellement' in the source.

columns.name: [X/H] random
columns.meaning: Random uncertainty in [X/H] related to the signal-to-noise ratio, derived as the difference between abundances from the two UVES spectra

columns.name: Nlines
columns.meaning: Number of spectral lines used or retained for the species (format sometimes shows e.g. 244/254, likely retained/total or used/retained)

columns.name: Teff
columns.meaning: Systematic uncertainty contribution from a temperature variation of ±100 K

columns.name: logg
columns.meaning: Systematic uncertainty contribution from a surface gravity variation of ±0.4 dex

columns.name: ξ
columns.meaning: Systematic uncertainty contribution from a microturbulence variation of ±0.2 km s⁻¹

internal_tables.evidence: LaTeX table in the appendix (label tab:sist) described in Appendix A as reporting uncertainties, with the second column being random uncertainty from S/N and the retained lines. Sec. 3.5 (Uncertainties) also references it.
internal_tables.comments: Header has a two-row structure: the second row clarifies that [X/H] is 'random', Teff is ±100 K, logg is ±0.4 dex, and ξ is ±0.2 km s⁻¹. Some cells are blank where data are not available.

external_resources.description: Online table at the Centre de Données astronomiques de Strasbourg (CDS) containing the spectral lines investigated in the analysis of GHS143.

source_refs.context: \thanks{The lines investigated are on an on-line table at CDS.}

external_resources.evidence: Explicitly mentioned in a title footnote that the investigated lines are available in an online table at CDS.
external_resources.comments: No specific CDS identifier or DOI given in the arXiv preprint. Not downloaded during review or extraction.

external_resources.description: Gaia Data Release 3 (DR3) astrometric and photometric data used for the initial selection and kinematic analysis of GHS143.

source_refs.context:
This work has made use of data from the European Space Agency (ESA) mission
{\it Gaia} (\url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/gaia}), processed by the {\it Gaia}
Data Processing and Analysis Consortium (DPAC,
\url{https://www.cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/dpac/consortium}).

external_resources.evidence: Acknowledged in the Acknowledgements section as the primary data source for astrometry and photometry.
external_resources.comments: General reference to the Gaia mission archive.
